revision.text:
'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう)は[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[佐比邇足尼]]。[[出雲氏]]の一族。[[成務]]朝にその孫の[[出雲笠夜命]]が島津国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[島氏]]。
=== 本拠 === 志摩国。現在の三重県志摩地方。
=== 氏神 === 不明。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)

== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[佐比邇足尼]]。[[出雲氏]]の一族。[[成務]]朝にその孫の[[出雲笠夜命]]が島津国造に任じられたという。

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう)は[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === 『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ）。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
{{DEFAULTSORT:しまつのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:島氏| ]] [[Category:出雲氏]] [[Category:志摩国| くにのみやつこ]] [[Category:三重県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=三重県}}

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう)は[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。 *「郷男爵家御系譜案」には出雲笠夜命と二方国造の祖・美尼布命を兄弟として伊賀曽熊命（伊加曽然命）の子としている<ref>[[中田憲信]]『郷男爵家御系譜案』。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされるが、[[志摩国]]には海産物献上の伝統や諏訪地方に見られる[[ミシャグジ|シャグジン信仰]]が存在しており、父とされる伊加曽然命の一族である神門臣が本来出雲臣とは別系の一族と見られることからも、島直の出自を[[大国主神]]の後裔で[[海人族]]の一派とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年。</ref>。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
{{DEFAULTSORT:しまつのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:島氏| ]] [[Category:出雲氏]] [[Category:志摩国| くにのみやつこ]] [[Category:三重県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=三重県}}

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう)は[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。 *「郷男爵家御系譜案」には出雲笠夜命と二方国造の祖・美尼布命を兄弟として伊賀曽熊命（伊加曽然命）の子としている<ref>[[中田憲信]]『郷男爵家御系譜案』。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされるが、[[志摩国]]には海産物献上の伝統や諏訪地方に見られる[[ミシャグジ|シャグジン信仰]]が存在しており、父とされる伊加曽然命の一族である神門臣が本来出雲臣とは別系の一族と見られることからも、島直の出自を[[大国主神]]の後裔で[[海人族]]の一派とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年。</ref>。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう)は[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。 *「郷男爵家御系譜案」には出雲笠夜命と二方国造の祖・美尼布命を兄弟として伊賀曽熊命（伊加曽然命）の子としている<ref>[[中田憲信]]『郷男爵家御系譜案』。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされるが、[[志摩国]]には海産物献上の伝統や諏訪地方に見られる[[ミシャグジ|シャグジン信仰]]が存在しており、父とされる伊加曽然命の一族である神門臣が本来出雲臣とは別系の一族と見られることからも、島直の出自を[[大国主神]]の後裔で[[海人族]]の一派とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年。</ref>。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう）は、[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。 *「郷男爵家御系譜案」には出雲笠夜命と二方国造の祖・美尼布命を兄弟として伊賀曽熊命（伊加曽然命）の子としている<ref>[[中田憲信]]『郷男爵家御系譜案』。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた<ref>[[太田亮]]「島」『姓氏家系大辞典』、1936年、2846頁。</ref>。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされるが、[[志摩国]]には海産物献上の伝統や諏訪地方に見られる[[ミシャグジ|シャグジン信仰]]が存在しており、父とされる伊加曽然命の一族である神門臣が本来出雲臣とは別系の一族と見られることからも、島直の出自を[[大国主神]]の後裔で[[海人族]]の一派とする説がある<ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年、193、194頁。</ref>。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
== 参考文献 == === 脚注 === {{Reflist}}
=== 参考文献 === *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店) *[[宝賀寿男]]『出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』（法令出版） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』第三巻（国民社）
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しまつのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:出雲氏]] [[Category:志摩国]] [[Category:三重県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=三重県}}

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう）は、[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。 *「郷男爵家御系譜案」には出雲笠夜命と二方国造の祖・美尼布命を兄弟として伊賀曽熊命（伊加曽然命）の子としている<ref>[[中田憲信]]『郷男爵家御系譜案』。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた<ref>[[太田亮]]「島」『姓氏家系大辞典』、1936年、2846頁。</ref>。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされるが、[[志摩国]]には海産物献上の伝統や諏訪地方に見られる[[ミシャグジ|シャグジン信仰]]が存在しており、父とされる伊加曽然命の一族である神門臣が本来出雲臣とは別系の一族と見られることからも、島直の出自を[[大国主神]]の後裔で[[海人族]]の一派とする説がある<ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年、193、194頁。</ref>。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
=== 関連神社 === * [[佐美長神社]]（さみながじんじゃ） *:[[志摩市]][[磯部町恵利原]]に鎮座する[[式内社]]。同族の[[二方国造]]と同じく[[大歳神]]を祀る<ref name="houga" />。
== 参考文献 == === 脚注 === {{Reflist}}
=== 参考文献 === *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店) *[[宝賀寿男]]『出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』（法令出版） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』第三巻（国民社）
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しまつのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:出雲氏]] [[Category:志摩国]] [[Category:三重県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=三重県}}

'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう）は、[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。 *「郷男爵家御系譜案」には出雲笠夜命と二方国造の祖・美尼布命を兄弟として伊賀曽熊命（伊加曽然命）の子としている<ref>[[中田憲信]]『郷男爵家御系譜案』。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた<ref>[[太田亮]]「島」『姓氏家系大辞典』、1936年、2846頁。</ref>。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされるが、[[志摩国]]には海産物献上の伝統や諏訪地方に見られる[[ミシャグジ|シャグジン信仰]]が存在しており、父とされる伊加曽然命の一族である神門臣が本来出雲臣とは別系の一族と見られることからも、島直の出自を[[大国主神]]の後裔で[[海人族]]の一派とする説がある<ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年、193、194頁。</ref>。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashin..."
revision.text:
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko}} [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Shima Province]] [[Category:History of Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:島津国造]]

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}

== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}

=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.

=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}

Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}

revision.text:
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha)== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
<!--Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->

<!--Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国|12=draft}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命|12=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼|12=draft}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏|12=draft}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直|12=draft}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=draft}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領|12=draft}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣|12=draft}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国|12=draft}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=draft}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=draft}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}}

Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領|12=draft}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣|12=draft}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国|12=draft}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=draft}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=draft}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} <!--Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->

'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国|12=draft}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命|12=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼|12=draft}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏|12=draft}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直|12=draft}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=draft}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領|12=draft}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣|12=draft}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|ja|志摩国|12=draft}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊雑宮|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} (highest-ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=draft}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|ja|佐美長神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=draft}}, they worshipped {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
=== Sources === * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoin) * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, ''Izumo-shi, Haji-shi: The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Hōrei Publishing) * Ryo Ota, ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' Vol. 3 (Kokuminsha) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} <!--Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->

15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:島津国造 up to rev 88406637
revision.text:
'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう）は、[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた<ref>[[太田亮]]「島」『姓氏家系大辞典』、1936年、2846頁。</ref>。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされる。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
=== 関連神社 === * [[佐美長神社]]（さみながじんじゃ） *:[[志摩市]][[磯部町恵利原]]に鎮座する[[式内社]]。同族の[[二方国造]]と同じく[[大歳神]]を祀る<ref name="houga" />。
== 参考文献 == === 脚注 === {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年、193、194頁。</ref> }}
=== 参考文献 === *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店) *[[宝賀寿男]]『出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』（法令出版） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』第三巻（国民社）
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[島津国造]] to [[Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''島津国造'''（しまづのくにのみやつこ・しまづこくぞう）は、[[志摩国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[出雲国造]]同祖・[[佐比邇足尼]]の孫の'''[[出雲笠夜命]]'''が島津国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === '''[[島氏]]'''（しまうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[二方国造]]と同族。国造制の終焉後も[[中世]]まで[[答志郡]][[郡領]]を務めた<ref>[[太田亮]]「島」『姓氏家系大辞典』、1936年、2846頁。</ref>。系図や「国造本紀」の記事から一般に[[出雲国造|出雲臣]]と同族とされる。
== 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''島（津）国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の'''[[志摩国]]'''、現在の[[三重県]][[志摩地方]]に当たる。
== 氏神 == 旧[[答志郡]]に鎮座する[[志摩国]][[一宮]]である'''[[伊雑宮]]'''。
=== 関連神社 === * [[佐美長神社]]（さみながじんじゃ） *:[[志摩市]][[磯部町恵利原]]に鎮座する[[式内社]]。同族の[[二方国造]]と同じく[[大歳神]]を祀る<ref name="houga" />。
== 参考文献 == === 脚注 === {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「五 崇神前代の出雲国の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑯ 出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』青垣出版、2020年、193、194頁。</ref> }}
=== 参考文献 === *『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本古代人名辭典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店) *[[宝賀寿男]]『出雲氏・土師氏 原出雲王国の盛衰』（法令出版） *[[太田亮]]『姓氏家系大辞典』第三巻（国民社）
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しまつのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:出雲氏|*しまつのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:志摩国]] [[Category:三重県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=三重県}}
