<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:島津国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:島津国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:島津国造]]
'''Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (島津国造, also read as Shimazu Kokuzō) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} (provincial governor) governing the {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}.
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" section), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja|出雲笠夜命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kasaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of {{ill|Sahini Ashini|ja|佐比邇足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahini Ashini|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was also an ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko belonged to the {{ill|Shima clan|ja|島氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shima-uji, with the title {{ill|Ason (title)|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason (title)|1=Q11581705|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}). They were related to the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}}. Even after the end of the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gunryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōshi District|1=Tōshi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603079}} during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima region|1=Shima region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490920}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Izawa-no-miya}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} (highest-Ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōshi District|1=Tōshi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603079}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|en|Saminaga Shrine|WD=Q17209205|lt=Saminaga Shrine|1=Saminaga Shrine}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe, Mie|1=Isobecho-Erihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588094}}, {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima City|1=Shima, Mie}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}}, they worshipped {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}

Even after the end of the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} system, they continued to serve as {{ill|gunryō|ja|郡領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gunryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}} (district governors) in {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōshi District|1=Tōshi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603079}} during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}}.<ref>Ryo Ota, "Shima," in ''Seishi Kakeizu Daijiten'' (1936), p. 2846.</ref> Based on genealogies and entries in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi,'' they are generally regarded as related to the {{ill|Izumo Omi|ja|出雲臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Territory == === Governed Region === The territory governed by the Shimazu Kuni no Miyatsuko corresponds to the area known in ancient times as "Shima (津) Province," later becoming {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|WD=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}, now the {{ill|Shima region|ja|志摩地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima region|1=Shima region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490920}} of {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.
== Patron Deity == The clan's deity is enshrined at the {{ill|Izawa-no-miya|en|Izawa-no-miya|WD=Q10885171|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Izawa-no-miya}}, the {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} (highest-Ranking shrine) of Shima Province, located in the former {{ill|Tōshi District|ja|答志郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōshi District|1=Tōshi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603079}}.
=== Associated Shrines === * {{ill|Saminaga Shrine|en|Saminaga Shrine|WD=Q17209205|lt=Saminaga Shrine|1=Saminaga Shrine}} *: Located in {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町恵利原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe, Mie|1=Isobecho-Erihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588094}}, {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima City|1=Shima, Mie}}, this is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Like their relatives the {{ill|Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}}, they worshipped {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="houga" />
== References == === Notes === {{reflist|refs= <ref name="houga">{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "Chapter 5: Developments in Izumo Province before Emperor Sujin," in ''Research on Ancient Clans ⑯: The Izumo and Haji Clans—The Rise and Fall of the Original Izumo Kingdom'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2020), pp. 193–194.</ref> }}
